body: Task: Fix the SQLite approval pending-ordering regression so the workspace can go green again. Keep ordering semantics deterministic across backends; if the correct rule is (created_at, approval_id) or similar, encode it in code/tests and update the assumption doc only if the rule was previously implicit. Success criteria: cargo test -p cairn-store --test cross_backend_parity --features sqlite passes, the sqlite_parity::approval_list_ordering_is_deterministic failure is gone, and you leave proof or an exact blocker. Relevant files: crates/cairn-store/tests/cross_backend_parity.rs, crates/cairn-store/src/in_memory.rs, crates/cairn-store/src/sqlite/adapter.rs, crates/cairn-store/src/sqlite/projections.rs, crates/cairn-store/BACKFILL_ASSUMPTIONS.md.
